title: Moquiniastrum ramboi
taxonomy: Moquiniastrum ramboi is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Brazil. The plant is part of the tribe Malveae. Moquiniastrum ramboi is a member of the genus Moquiniastrum, which is classified within the family Malvaceae. The classification of Moquiniastrum ramboi is further detailed in its ranking as a species within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Moquiniastrum ramboi, also known as Rambo's Moquiniastrum, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Moquiniastrum ramboi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
